messages.content: the seasons are caused by the Earth being closer or further from the sun is false now covert this statement into true
The seasons are caused by the tilt of the Earth's axis relative to its orbit around the sun.
the earth orbits around the sun in a perfectly circular pathway true or false
False - The Earth's orbit around the sun is not perfectly circular; it is slightly elliptical.
the earth orbits around the sun in a perfectly circular pathway is false now covert this statement into true
The Earth's orbit around the sun is slightly elliptical, meaning it is not a perfect circle.
the seasons are caused because the earth is tilted on it's axis(23.5 degrees) true or false
True - The seasons are caused because the Earth is tilted on its axis at an angle of approximately 23.5 degrees.
The earths hemisphere receive direct sunlight in the summer, and indirect sunlight during the winter

True - The Earth's hemisphere receives direct sunlight and is tilted towards the sun during summer, while it receives indirect sunlight and is tilted away from the sun during winter. This causes the variation in temperature and other climatic conditions leading to the seasonal changes.
complete the sentence: The change in seasons is cause _ by the _ in the Earth's _.
The change in seasons is caused by the tilt in the Earth's axis.
The two halves of the Earth are called North and South _. Dubai is in the _ hemisphere
The two halves of the Earth are called North and South Hemispheres. Dubai is in the Northern Hemisphere.
Because of the tilt, one _ point towards the sun and one point
Because of the tilt, one hemisphere points towards the sun and one hemisphere points away from the sun, resulting in the change of seasons.
Because of the tilt, one hemisphere points towards the sun and one hemisphere points towards the sun and one points _. it is the summer in the hemisphere that points _ the sun,

Because of the tilt, one hemisphere points towards the sun and one hemisphere points away from the sun. It is the summer in the hemisphere that points towards the sun (Northern Hemisphere in June, July, and August), while it is winter in the hemisphere that points away from the sun (Southern Hemisphere in December, January, and February).
